reference_resolution.declared_rate_note: refs.yaml marks 17/19 refs resolvable=true (R01-R03, R06-R19; R04, R05 external_id=not_specified). Sample here restricted to the 12 refs with a DOI resolvable via PubMed search (R01,R02,R06,R07,R08,R09,R10,R11,R12,R13,R14,R15); R03 (EMC SmPC URL) and R04/R05 (EMA docs, no external_id) were excluded as non-PubMed-indexable, not because they failed resolution.

claims.claim_summary: iADRS CFB diff 2.32 [0.69,3.95], p=0.005, 17.9% slowing, EU-eligible population.
claims.note: Sims 2023 JAMA (PMID 37459141) already reported the iADRS treatment effect for the overall and low/medium-tau TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 populations (diff 2.92 and 3.25 respectively). This ARA recuts the same trial to the EU-eligible (APOE e4 non-carrier/heterozygote, no siderosis/anticoagulant/uncontrolled HTN) subgroup with a different imputation method -- the specific number (2.32) and population are new, but the qualitative finding (donanemab slows iADRS decline) is not.

claims.claim_summary: CDR-SB CFB diff -0.67 [-0.97,-0.37], p<0.001, 28.2% slowing, EU-eligible population.
claims.note: Sims 2023 JAMA already reported a CDR-SB difference of -0.67 [-0.95,-0.40] for the low/medium-tau population and -0.7 for combined population -- coincidentally near-identical point estimate to this EU-eligible subgroup result, but for a differently defined subgroup (tau level vs. APOE/contraindication eligibility). Not the same population, so not classified 'previously_reported', but the direction/magnitude of effect was already well established.

claims.claim_summary: 40.3% lower risk of CDR-Global progression (HR 0.597) and 47.2% lower risk to moderate dementia (HR 0.528), EU-eligible population, n=581.
claims.note: No prior PubMed record found reporting CDR-Global stage-progression hazard ratios specifically for the EU-eligible donanemab subgroup. Searches for 'donanemab risk reduction progression CDR global hazard ratio' returned no clean hits distinct from this paper.

claims.claim_summary: ARIA-E 19.5%, IRR 8.0%, no ARIA-related deaths in EU-eligible population (n=609); vs 36.8% any ARIA in overall trial population.
claims.note: Sims 2023 JAMA already reported overall-population ARIA-E (24.0%) and IRR (8.7%) rates for TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2. This paper's EU-eligible-restricted rates (19.5% / 8.0%, lower than overall) are a new subgroup cut of previously-reported safety data, consistent with the expected lower-ARIA profile in non-carriers/heterozygotes already flagged in EMA labeling discussions (R04/R05, not independently verifiable via PubMed).

claims.claim_summary: LTE treatment benefit increases over 154 weeks; early-start vs delayed-start 29% lower CDR-Global progression risk; amyloid clearance maintained post-treatment.
claims.note: Zimmer et al. 2025 (PMID 41330788, J Prev Alzheimers Dis) already reported the TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 LTE at 3 years for the general (non-EU-restricted) population, including an early- vs delayed-start CDR-Global hazard ratio of 0.73 (27% risk reduction) vs a propensity-weighted ADNI external control, and maintained amyloid clearance. This ARA's claim (29% risk reduction) is the same design/comparator recut to the EU-eligible subgroup -- an incremental extension, not a new finding type.

registry.trial_title: Assessment of Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy of Donanemab in Early Symptomatic Alzheimer's Disease (TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2)

Change From Baseline on the Integrated Alzheimer's Disease Rating Scale (iADRS) (Intermediate (Low-medium) Tau Population)
registered_secondary: Change From Baseline on the Mini Mental State Examination (MMSE) Score (Overall Population)
Change From Baseline on the Mini Mental State Examination (MMSE) Score (Intermediate (Low-medium) Tau Population)
Change From Baseline on the Alzheimer's Disease Assessment Scale - Cognitive Subscale (ADAS-Cog13) (Overall Population)
Change From Baseline on the Alzheimer's Disease Assessment Scale - Cognitive Subscale (ADAS-Cog13) (Intermediate (Low-medium) Tau Population)
Change From Baseline on the Clinical Dementia Rating Scale-Sum of Boxes (CDR-SB) (Overall Population)
Change From Baseline on the Clinical Dementia Rating Scale-Sum of Boxes (CDR-SB) (Intermediate (Low-medium) Tau Population)
Change From Baseline on the Alzheimer's Disease Cooperative Study - Instrumental Activities of Daily Living (ADCS-iADL) Score (Overall Population)
Change From Baseline on the Alzheimer's Disease Cooperative Study - Instrumental Activities of Daily Living (ADCS-iADL) Score (Intermediate (Low-medium) Tau Population)
Change From Baseline in Brain Amyloid Plaque Deposition as Measured by Amyloid Positron Emission Tomography (PET) Scan
Change From Baseline in Brain Tau Deposition as Measured by Flortaucipir F18 PET Scan

Pharmacokinetics (PK): Average Serum Concentration at Steady State of Donanemab
Number or Participants With Anti-Donanemab Antibodies
reported: iADRS CFB difference (C01) -- matches registered primary outcome (iADRS), applied to a new post-hoc subgroup (EU-eligible) not in the registered population strata.

CDR-SB CFB difference, domain breakdown, no-progression proportions, time-saved (C02, C04, C05, C06) -- matches registered secondary outcome (CDR-SB), with derived metrics (domain analysis, no-progression, time-saved) not themselves separately registered.

CDR-Global stage progression hazard ratios (C03) -- CDR-Global time-to-event/Cox progression analysis is NOT among the registered primary/secondary outcome measures for NCT04437511 (registry lists CDR-SB change-from-baseline, not CDR-Global stage transition).

Amyloid PET centiloid reduction and clearance rates, plasma P-tau217 (C07) -- amyloid PET matches a registered secondary outcome; plasma P-tau217 is NOT registered (registry lists tau PET via flortaucipir F18, not plasma P-tau217 biomarker).

ARIA-E/ARIA-H incidence, infusion-related reactions, deaths (C08) -- these are safety/adverse-event data, not listed among the registered efficacy outcome measures fetched here (ClinicalTrials.gov typically reports AEs in a separate results module not covered by this outcome-measures fetch).

LTE 154-week early-start vs delayed-start comparison against propensity-weighted ADNI external control (C09) -- the long-term extension design and its external-control comparison are not part of the original registered outcome measures for the placebo-controlled main study period.

concordance.outcome: LTE early-start vs delayed-start vs ADNI external control (154 wk)
ADAS-Cog13, MMSE, ADCS-iADL, tau PET, vMRI, PK, immunogenicity (registered secondary outcomes)

registry.concordance_note: This ARA is explicitly titled and framed as a 'post-hoc analysis' of TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2, restricted to a regulatory-defined EU-eligible subgroup with a different (conservative hybrid J2R/CIR) imputation method than the original registered analysis, plus new derived endpoints (CDR-Global progression, time-saved, no-progression proportions) and a long-term-extension comparison against an external ADNI control not part of the original trial design. This is disclosed by the paper itself, not concealed outcome-switching -- but by definition none of the CDR-Global, P-tau217, time-saved, or LTE-external-control comparisons were pre-registered outcome measures for NCT04437511, and several registered secondary outcomes (ADAS-Cog13, MMSE, ADCS-iADL, tau PET, vMRI, PK, immunogenicity) are not reported in this ARA at all.

checked: This paper itself (10.1016/j.tjpad.2026.100605 / PMID 42202469): indexed in PubMed with no retraction/erratum publication-type flag.
Mintun et al. 2021 NEJM (PMID 33720637): no retraction/erratum flag.
Sims et al. 2023 JAMA (PMID 37459141): no retraction/erratum flag.
Cogswell et al. 2025 AJNR (PMID 39179297): no retraction/erratum flag.
Zimmer et al. 2025 LTE (PMID 41330788): no retraction/erratum flag.
PubMed searches for 'donanemab[Title] AND "Retraction of Publication"[Publication Type]' and 'TRAILBLAZER-ALZ[Title/Abstract] AND "Published Erratum"[Publication Type]' returned zero hits.
retractions.notes: No retractions or corrections found for this paper or its most-cited references, as expected for a 2026 industry-authored post-hoc analysis with no reported integrity issues.

notes: All 12 sampled DOI-bearing refs (of 19 total in refs.yaml; 17 declared resolvable, 2 declared not-resolvable/not_specified) resolved cleanly to a single PubMed record each with titles/authors matching the citation text -- observed_resolvability 1.0 for the DOI-bearing subset, consistent with (in fact exceeding, since the excluded refs were non-PubMed URL/regulatory-doc citations rather than failed DOIs) the sidecar's declared 0.895 rate. The paper's own headline numbers are independently confirmed via its own now-indexed PubMed record (PMID 42202469, published 2026-05-27) -- the ARA accurately transcribes the abstract's key figures (CDR-SB diff -0.7 [-1.0,-0.4], 40.3% CDR-Global risk reduction, ARIA-E 119/609=19.5%, IRR 49/609=8.0%). Novelty assessment is conservative: nearly all headline claims are subgroup-specific recuts of effects already established in the pivotal Sims 2023 JAMA report and the Zimmer 2025 LTE report for the general trial population, so 'incremental' rather than 'previously_reported' (population differs) or 'novel' (finding-type is not new) is the modal verdict; only the CDR-Global-progression-specific EU-eligible HR was not locatable in any prior PubMed record. Registry concordance shows the paper is transparent about being a post-hoc, non-multiplicity-controlled subgroup analysis with new derived endpoints (CDR-Global progression, time-saved, LTE-vs-ADNI) that were never part of the original trial registration -- this is disclosed exploratory-analysis outcome-switching, not concealed switching, but it is real by the letter of the registered outcome measures.
